questions.question: Why are primitive types like int or char forbidden from being passed directly as parameters to Java Generics?
answers: Database drivers cannot bind primitive types to statement query parameters
The modularity guidelines written in module-info block primitive parameters
Generics require object references on the heap; primitives do not inherit from Object
Primitives consume too much L1 cache memory causing thread scheduler stalls

questions.question: What primary objective does Java's Type Erasure accomplish when compiling generic code architectures?
answers: It forces the compiler to duplicate class files for every unique type
It restricts collection instances from scaling horizontally
It replaces type parameters with bounds or Object to ensure type safety without runtime overhead
It converts object reference addresses into encrypted byte arrays

questions.question: What does the wildcard pattern 'List<? super Integer>' signify inside type boundaries checking?
answers: It specifies a List that can hold Integer or any of its master superclass ancestor layers
It tells the system that the collection properties are bound to an index
It limits the collection strictly to elements that implement serializable
It prevents downstream functions from executing list mutations

questions.question: What happens when you use a raw type instead of a parameterized generic type declaration?
answers: You lose compile-time type safety checking benefits and trigger compiler warning logs
Jackson's ObjectMapper becomes unable to parse JSON resource file streams
The application crashes instantly with a ClassCastException upon boot paths
The Java Virtual Machine executes the bytecode lines 50 percent slower

questions.question: Which pillar of OOP allows a subclass to provide a specific implementation of a method defined in its superclass?
answers: Inheritance (Method Overloading)
Polymorphism (Method Overriding)
Encapsulation
Abstraction execution rules

questions.question: What does a class inherit when it implements a Java Interface containing default methods?
answers: The concrete private instance fields
The interface's default functional implementations without breaking backward compatibility
Complete access to the abstract module's private memory
Automated thread synchronization parameters across loop executions

questions.question: What block in a try-catch-finally layout is guaranteed to execute regardless of whether an exception is thrown?
answers: The throws block
The catch block
The try block
The finally block

questions.question: What happens if a catch block intercepts an exception and rethrows a new RuntimeException wrapper?
answers: The active relational database transaction commits its row status
The current execution path breaks and the exception propagates up the thread stack frame
The operating system kernel terminates the parent thread launcher instance
The JVM defaults all text inputs back to empty strings

questions.question: How does the try-with-resources statement handle asset hygiene automatically on block exits?
answers: It automatically invokes .close() on any resource object that implements AutoCloseable
It saves an execution checkpoint status state directly back to tables
It flushes the CPU cache logs to clear remaining variables
It dispatches a dedicated Garbage Collection sweep task

questions.question: What type of exception is a NullPointerException classified as inside the Java runtime architecture?
answers: A Checked Exception
An Unchecked Exception (RuntimeException)
An Error exception condition
A Compile-time syntax flag anomaly
